title: ECL: Langlands Bypass Strategy

bypass.object: Langlands bypass: proving RH without ECL
bypass.key_idea: If ζ is automorphic (Langlands for GL(1)/Q = class field theory), then zeros = spectral eigenvalues; spectral unitarity forces Re=1/2.

steps.step1: GL(1) Langlands for Q = class field theory: ESTABLISHED
steps.step2: ζ(s) = L(s, trivial character) = automorphic L-function for GL(1)
steps.step3: Automorphic L-functions have functional equation and Euler product: ✓
steps.step4: Spectral interpretation: zeros = eigenvalues of Hecke operators on L²(GL(1,A))
steps.step5: Hecke operators are self-adjoint (unitary): eigenvalues have |λ|=1 → Re=1/2?
steps.gap: Step 5: 'Hecke eigenvalues → zero locations' needs explicit bridge

selberg.object: Why ECL holds for Selberg: reverse-engineering the proof

selberg_ecl.setup: Selberg zeta Z_Γ(s): zeros = spectral eigenvalues of Laplacian Δ on Γ\H
selberg_ecl.why_ecl_holds: Δ is self-adjoint: eigenvalues λ_n = 1/4 + r_n² are real
selberg_ecl.zeros: s_n = 1/2 + ir_n: purely on Re=1/2 because λ_n real
selberg_ecl.et_constancy: ET(Z_Γ(s)) = 3 because exp(ir_n): imaginary eigenvalue part
selberg_ecl.key_property: Self-adjointness of Δ → real eigenvalues → zeros on line

transfer_to_zeta.question: What self-adjoint operator has ζ(s) as spectral determinant?
transfer_to_zeta.hilbert_polya: Hilbert-Pólya: such operator exists but explicit form unknown
transfer_to_zeta.connes_approach: Connes: adelic operators on L²(A/Q): promising but incomplete
transfer_to_zeta.eml_version: If H exists with ET(H)=2 (self-adjoint, real): zeros from ET(H)=2 side have ET=3 by Langlands split!
transfer_to_zeta.new_insight: LANGLANDS BYPASS: if H(EML-2, self-adjoint) then spectrum(EML-3) by Langlands split → zeros on Re=1/2

gl1.object: GL(1) Langlands (class field theory) and ζ zeros

global_langlands_gl1.statement: Hecke characters (Grössencharacters) ↔ GL(1,A) automorphic reps: PROVEN
global_langlands_gl1.depth: L(s,1) = EML-3 (automorphic = oscillatory by Langlands split)

hecke_operators.T_p: Hecke operator T_p on automorphic forms: self-adjoint on L²
hecke_operators.eigenvalues: T_p eigenvalues for trivial character: 1+p^{-s}? Needs explicit calculation
hecke_operators.unitarity: Unitary group action: eigenvalues on unit circle = exp(i·θ) = EML-3

bypass_sketch.claim: Spectral unitarity of GL(1,A) action → Hecke eigenvalues on unit circle → zeros on Re=1/2
bypass_sketch.status: Sketch; bridge from eigenvalues to zeros needs formalization
bypass_sketch.progress: Stronger than S316 sketch: uses global Langlands (established)

function_field.object: Transferring the function field ECL mechanism to classical ζ

function_field.frobenius: Frobenius Fr_q acting on H^1_et(C, Q_l)
function_field.rh_proof: Weil: eigenvalues of Fr_q have |α| = q^{1/2} → zeros on Re=1/2
function_field.why_ecl_trivial: Fr_q is an algebraic operator: ET = 3 BY CONSTRUCTION (eigenvectors in Q_l(complex))

classical_analog.analogy: Fr_q ↔ Frobenius-like operator on classical ζ?
classical_analog.obstacle: No natural Frobenius operator for Q (infinitely many primes)
classical_analog.connes: Connes' approach: adelic Frobenius Fr acting on adelic space
classical_analog.eml_diagnosis: Function field: ET=3 by construction (algebraic); classical: ET=3 needs proof
classical_analog.key_difference: FUNCTION FIELD HAS EXPLICIT EML-3 OPERATOR; classical ζ DOES NOT YET

verdicts.bypass: Langlands bypass valid in principle; bridge step 4→5 needs formalization
verdicts.selberg_key: Selberg: self-adjoint Δ → real eigenvalues → zeros on line; ζ needs explicit H
verdicts.gl1: GL(1) Langlands established; spectral unitarity → zeros sketch
verdicts.function_field: Function field: ET=3 by construction; classical ζ needs this construction
verdicts.new_insight: Key: find explicit EML-3 operator for ζ; function field shows it EXISTS

key_theorem: Langlands Bypass Insight (S347): The Selberg trace formula proof works because Δ is explicitly self-adjoint (EML-2): real eigenvalues → zeros on Re=1/2 directly. Function field: Frobenius Fr is an explicit EML-3 operator BY CONSTRUCTION. Classical ζ: no explicit EML-3 operator known yet (this is the Hilbert-Pólya problem). NEW: Langlands bypass sketch — GL(1) Langlands (established) + spectral unitarity gives zeros on Re=1/2 IF the bridge from Hecke eigenvalues to zero locations is formalized. The Langlands bypass reduces RH to: find the explicit Hilbert-Pólya operator for ζ.
rabbit_hole_log: Selberg: self-adjoint Δ(EML-2) → spectrum(EML-3) → zeros on line
Function field: explicit EML-3 operator (Frobenius) makes ECL trivial
Classical ζ: no explicit EML-3 operator = Hilbert-Pólya problem
GL(1) Langlands: established; spectral unitarity sketch
NEW: RH reduces to finding explicit H(EML-2) with ζ as spectral determinant
